instructions.objective: Use multishot accept (5.19+), multishot recv (6.0+), and send zero-copy (6.0+) to reduce syscalls and improve throughput.
instructions.constraints: - Graceful fallback to single-shot on older kernels - Handle CQE overflow correctly - Integrate with buffer pooling (issue #28) - Cancellation safety (verify with issue #26) - Target: >20% RPS improvement

context.tree: harrow-server-monoio/ ├── src/ │ ├── lib.rs # Accept loop here │ ├── connection.rs # Read/write operations │ └── codec.rs └── tests/ └── integration.rs

guidance.notes:
Key changes:
1. lib.rs accept loop (lines 98-132): - Replace listener.accept() with multishot_accept - Each CQE spawns a connection handler - Handle ring saturation
2. connection.rs read_headers/read_body: - Use recv_multishot instead of repeated read() calls - Kernel fills provided buffer ring, we parse
3. connection.rs write_response: - Use send_zc for large bodies - Fallback to write_all for small responses
Depends on: Issue #28 (buffer pool) for provided buffers
